summary: G1 FAIL: 6/12 workflows have `actions/checkout11bd...` (missing `@` symbol) — every checkouts fails to resolve. G3 PARTIAL: trufflehog pinned to tag, not SHA (tick-17 rot). G4 FAIL: no real `cargo test`/`cargo clippy` job anywhere; only echo stubs with `continue-on-error: true`. G2/G5/e2e otherwise solid. 3 actionable findings, P0=1.

evidence: 6/12 workflows have malformed `uses: actions/checkout11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683` (missing `@` between action name and SHA) — line refs: cargo-machete.yml:21, cargo-semver-checks.yml:16, doc-links.yml:11, fr-coverage.yml:11, quality-gate.yml:12, scorecard.yml:26. Every one of these jobs is non-functional: GitHub resolves the literal string `actions/checkout11bd...` and fails with 'Could not find action'.

6/12 correct (with `@`): cargo-audit.yml:24, cargo-deny.yml:23 (uses v6 SHA `de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd`, drift from v4 SHAs in the other 11), codeql-rust.yml:27, codeql.yml:31, journey-gate.yml:62, trufflehog.yml:15.
No 'Not Found' JSON corruption and no `$123...125` expression rot (this repo is clean of the tick-22 corruption pattern).
No trufflehog/actions rot (fixed per PR #41) — but trufflehog.yml:17 still uses `trufflesecurity/trufflehog@v3.88.27` (mutable tag, not SHA-pinned).
Permissions block missing in 8/12 workflows: cargo-deny, cargo-machete, cargo-semver-checks, doc-links, fr-coverage, journey-gate, quality-gate, trufflehog. Concurrency declared in 12/12 (good).
LICENSE (MIT), CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, FUNDING.yml, CITATION.cff all present.
.github/CODEOWNERS present with @KooshaPari default + .github/workflows/ rule (correct location, not at root).
.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (org-stub) + ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug_report.md, config.yml, feature_request.md} (org-standard 3-file set).
SUPPORT.md MISSING (only org-mandated community-health file gap).
AGENTS.md header matches repo name `rich-cli-kit`; module path consistent. No naming drift.
trufflehog.yml: present (push+pull_request) but uses `trufflesecurity/trufflehog@v3.88.27` (tag, not SHA) — tick-17 SHA-pinning rot. Org-canonical SHA: `75add79b929b263dae147d2e5bcf0daf292165cf`.
scorecard.yml: cron 17 3 * * 6, permissions: read-all, security-events/id-token write — well-shaped.
codeql-rust.yml: cron 17 4 * * 2, matrix `rust` only (correct for a Rust-only repo). CodeQL-Python `codeql.yml` also present (covers mcp/ Python subdir).
cargo-audit.yml: cron 0 2 * * 3, push to main, PR — but no permissions block.
cargo-deny.yml: cron present (per STATUS.md), no `permissions:` (G1c).
Renovate (renovate.json5) + Dependabot (dependabot.yml) BOTH configured for cargo + github-actions ecosystems (redundant but not broken).

NO workflow runs `cargo test`, `cargo build`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, or `cargo fmt --check`. The full local CI sweep is in justfile (line 39: `ci: lint test audit unused`) and Taskfile.yml (task: ci), but neither runs in CI.
quality-gate.yml:12-13 has the broken checkout + a single `run: echo "Quality gate check (phenotype-tooling integration)"` step with `continue-on-error: true`. Pure stub.
fr-coverage.yml:10-11 same pattern: broken checkout + `echo "FR coverage check..."` stub. No FR mapping anywhere.
doc-links.yml: broken checkout only.

Pre-commit (.pre-commit-config.yaml) calls `cargo fmt` (via doublify/pre-commit-rust) and `cargo check` locally — but not clippy. justfile/Taskfile are the only real test/lint runners and they run client-side only.
deny.toml + clippy.toml + rustfmt.toml + rust-toolchain.toml (stable + clippy + rustfmt components) all present — config is good, just no CI job wires them.
renovate.json5 + dependabot.yml: cargo (weekly Tuesday) + github-actions (weekly Monday).
PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md: org-stub with Summary/Type-of-Change/Testing/Documentation sections.
ISSUE_TEMPLATE/: 3-file org set (bug_report.md, config.yml, feature_request.md).
.coderabbit.yaml MISSING (org-mandated). .gemini/ MISSING (org-mandated).
CHANGELOG.md present (cliff.toml uses git-cliff). cliff.toml:1 looks well-formed.
Local main 0 commits ahead of origin/main (a83f352 = 'docs: add one-line install to README #43'). Clean working tree.

tests/smoke_test.rs:9-14 is a single `#[test] fn smoke_test() { assert_eq!(2+2, 4, ...) }` — does not exercise rck-core or rck-cli. No unit tests inside crates/rck-core/src/*.rs or crates/rck-cli/src/main.rs (no `#[cfg(test)]` blocks found in 1891 LOC of source).
mcp/tests/test_rck_wrapper.py: exists (Python wrapper test) but no pytest step in any CI workflow.
No e2e/ or integration/ subdirs. No harness, no golden-output, no kitty-graphics-protocol fixture tests.
Consequence: the only 'CI' that runs is scorecard + codeql + trufflehog + cargo-audit/deny/machete/semver — all of which compile but don't run any test. Build correctness is unverified in CI.

actions_recommended.title: Restore the missing `@` in 6/12 workflow files where `actions/checkout<sha>` is missing the separator
actions_recommended.detail: Files: cargo-machete.yml:21, cargo-semver-checks.yml:16, doc-links.yml:11, fr-coverage.yml:12, quality-gate.yml:12, scorecard.yml:26. The literal string `actions/checkout11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683` is not a valid `uses:` ref — must be `actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683`. Once restored, every workflow resolves. Cargo-deny.yml:23 uses a different SHA (`de0fac2e...`) labelled v6 — verify it's the org-canonical v6 SHA before merging; if not, normalize to v4 `11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683` for consistency.
actions_recommended.estimated_effort: 5 min: sed -i '' 's|actions/checkout11bd|actions/checkout@11bd|g' on 6 files, then `actionlint` or push + watch the green CI

actions_recommended.title: Pin trufflehog action to the org-canonical SHA and add a real Rust CI job (cargo test + cargo clippy -D warnings)
actions_recommended.detail: trufflehog.yml:17 — replace `trufflesecurity/trufflehog@v3.88.27` with `trufflesecurity/trufflehog@75add79b929b263dae147d2e5bcf0daf292165cf` (org-canonical per tick-17 SSOT). Then add a new `ci.yml` that runs the sweep that justfile:39 already defines: `cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace --all-features`. This is the missing gate — without it, the repo has no test coverage in CI at all (smoke_test.rs is `2+2=4`; no other tests exist in 1891 LOC of source).
actions_recommended.estimated_effort: 45 min: 1-line SHA swap + 40-line new ci.yml that mirrors the justfile ci target

actions_recommended.title: Add SUPPORT.md, .coderabbit.yaml, and .gemini/ to close the org-mandated community-health gap
actions_recommended.detail: Per the audit pattern in argis-extensions and phenodocs, the three org-mandated files missing here are SUPPORT.md (12-line org-stub), .coderabbit.yaml (review-bot config with path filters and tool allowlist), and .gemini/config.yaml. Also add `permissions: read-all` (or a per-job permissions block) to the 8/12 workflows currently missing it (cargo-deny, cargo-machete, cargo-semver-checks, doc-links, fr-coverage, journey-gate, quality-gate, trufflehog). The mcp/ Python subproject would also benefit from a `mcp/.github/workflows/python-ci.yml` running `pytest mcp/tests/` — currently zero Python CI exists.
actions_recommended.estimated_effort: 30 min: 3 stub files + permissions-block sed + 1 new mcp python-ci workflow

deep_research: Rich-cli-kit is a small but well-shaped Rust workspace (Cargo.toml:6 rust-version=1.75, edition 2021) that ships two crates — `rck-core` (capability detection + kitty-graphics encoder, 11 files, ~1.6k LOC) and `rck-cli` (clap-driven `rck` binary with subcommands detect | image | progress | panel | demo) — plus a Python FastMCP server in `mcp/rich_cli_mcp/` (rck.py + server.py) and a `shaders/` directory. It is the visual-output counterpart to the phenotype-* repos that focus on data and orchestration, and the architecture is sound: kitty-graphics-protocol emit falls back to plain ASCII, MCP exposure is one-to-one with the CLI surface, and rust-toolchain.toml pins stable + clippy + rustfmt. Compared to its peers (PhenoKits, phenodocs, PhenoRuntime), rich-cli-kit has the cleanest naming/identity (AGENTS.md, repo, and module path all agree on `rich-cli-kit`; no bifrost-style drift) and the smallest community-health gap (only SUPPORT.md, .coderabbit.yaml, .gemini/ missing — three files vs argis-extensions' four). The repo also escapes the tick-22 workflow-corruption pattern (no `Not Found` JSON objects, no `$123...125` placeholders) and the tick-17 trufflehog tag-vs-SHA pattern is partially fixed in PR #41 (trufflehog/actions/setup replaced) but the tag-vs-SHA pin issue in trufflehog.yml:17 is still open.
deep_research_cont: However, rich-cli-kit has a unique and severe G1 failure that none of its siblings have: 6 of 12 workflows have a literal typo where `actions/checkout<sha>` is missing the `@` separator (cargo-machete.yml:21, cargo-semver-checks.yml:16, doc-links.yml:11, fr-coverage.yml:12, quality-gate.yml:12, scorecard.yml:26). The other 6 workflows (cargo-audit.yml, cargo-deny.yml [v6 SHA], codeql-rust.yml, codeql.yml, journey-gate.yml, trufflehog.yml) use the correct form. The typo is mechanical to fix (one sed) and is the single highest-leverage change: it unblocks 6 CI jobs in one PR. Layered on top of G1 is G4 — the repo has zero `cargo test` or `cargo clippy` jobs in CI, only `continue-on-error: true` echo stubs in quality-gate.yml and fr-coverage.yml; the only real test (tests/smoke_test.rs:9) is `assert_eq!(2+2, 4)`, and 1891 LOC of source have no `#[cfg(test)]` blocks. The combination means the repo has CI presence without CI substance — every gate runs but nothing actually verifies the code works. E2e is degraded: no e2e/ or integration/ dir, no golden-output fixture for the kitty-graphics encoder, no round-trip test for the MCP wrapper. Hygiene score: 2.4/5 (G1 fail, G4 fail, G3/G5 partial, G2 pass).
